Title: Deciphering triterpenoid saponin biosynthesis by leveraging transcriptome response to methyl jasmonate elicitation in Saponaria vaccaria

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tested in reconstructed downstream C28 sugar-chain pathways
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Could not glycosylate QA-C3-GlcA-C28-Fuc, but could add a hexose or deoxyhexose to elongated C28 sugar-chain substrates QA-C3-GlcA-C28-Fuc-Rha and QA-C3-GlcA-C28-Fuc-Rha-Xyl
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient combinatorial expression in N. benthamiana with heterologous C28 chain-building enzymes and LC-MS
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Q. saponaria C28 rhamnosyltransferase; Q. saponaria C28 xylosyltransferase
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PB.29740.3 was inactive on QA-C3-GlcA-C28-Fuc. 2. It glycosylated more elaborated C28 sugar-chain substrates with a hexose or deoxyhexose. 3. It likely participates in late C28 sugar-chain diversification of S. vaccaria saponins.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potentially useful for terminal diversification of C28 sugar chains in pathway engineering once upstream chain-building steps are installed
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Sugar identity and linkage position remain unresolved
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Hexose/deoxyhexose are proposed to be glucose and D-fucose based on known S. vaccaria structures; requires further confirmation.

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tested in reconstructed downstream C28 sugar-chain pathways
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Did not glycosylate QA-C3-GlcA-C28-Fuc or QA-C3-GlcA-C28-Fuc-Rha, but glycosylated QA-C3-GlcA-C28-Fuc-Rha-Xyl with a hexose or deoxyhexose
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient combinatorial expression in N. benthamiana with heterologous C28 chain-building enzymes and LC-MS
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Q. saponaria C28 rhamnosyltransferase; Q. saponaria C28 xylosyltransferase
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PB.17537.3 acts late in C28 sugar-chain elaboration. 2. Activity was observed only on QA-C3-GlcA-C28-Fuc-Rha-Xyl. 3. It contributes to diversification of mature bisdesmoside C28 oligosaccharides.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate late-stage decorating enzyme for reconstituting native-like complex saponins
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Exact transferred sugar and linkage remain unresolved
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Product retention time differences suggest a distinct glycosylation position from PB.29740.3.

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tested in reconstructed downstream C28 sugar-chain pathways
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Did not glycosylate QA-C3-GlcA-C28-Fuc or QA-C3-GlcA-C28-Fuc-Rha-Xyl with hexose/deoxyhexose, but added a pentose to QA-C3-GlcA-C28-Fuc-Rha-Xyl
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient combinatorial expression in N. benthamiana with heterologous C28 chain-building enzymes and LC-MS
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Q. saponaria C28 rhamnosyltransferase; Q. saponaria C28 xylosyltransferase
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PB.33723.2 appears to add a pentose during late C28 sugar-chain extension. 2. The likely pentose is L-arabinofuranose based on known S. vaccaria saponin structures. 3. It contributes to terminal diversification of the C28 oligosaccharide.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for late-stage engineering of complex C28 oligosaccharide decorations if substrate pathway is already assembled
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Transferred sugar identity and exact linkage position remain unconfirmed
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional assignment is tentative and requires structural confirmation.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Saponaria vaccaria; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Saponaria vaccaria; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GAL promoters in yeast for pathway reconstitution; transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana; endogenous promoter in S. vaccaria
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 μM methyl jasmonate strongly induced expression in leaves and flowers; hydroponic growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in leaves and flowers after MeJA treatment, highest induction at 24 h with 100 μM MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In S. vaccaria leaves and flowers after MeJA; transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves for pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: First committed step of oleanane-type triterpenoid saponin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies core triterpene scaffold precursor for mono- and bisdesmosidic saponins
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Oleanane-type triterpenoid saponins including vaccaroside E, segetoside I, quillaic acid- and gypsogenic acid-derived saponins
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots; stems; leaves; flowers; medicinally important seeds mentioned in background
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes production of β-amyrin-derived triterpenoid saponin pathway intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR induction analysis; transient pathway expression in N. benthamiana; yeast pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SvC28 oxidase; SvC16 oxidase; SvC23-1; SvC23-2; SvCslG; AtATR1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio Iso-Seq BioProject PRJNA951399; Illumina 3'-Tag-RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA949801
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SvβAS was strongly upregulated by MeJA in leaves and flowers. 2. It defines the first committed step into oleanane-type triterpenoid saponin biosynthesis. 3. Co-expression with downstream oxidases enabled reconstitution of aglycone pathways in heterologous hosts.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core entry-point gene for reconstructing and boosting triterpenoid saponin pathways in plants and microbes; useful for pathway stacking with oxidases and glycosyltransferases
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as bait/reference gene for co-expression mining of downstream pathway genes.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Saponaria vaccaria
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Saponaria vaccaria
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana; GAL promoters in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Candidate identified through MeJA co-upregulation with SvβAS
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-upregulated with SvβAS by MeJA in leaves and flowers
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream oxidation step after scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key aglycone tailoring step; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes β-amyrin at C28 to form oleanolic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms major oxidized aglycone precursor for downstream C16/C23 oxidation and glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables C28 oxidation required for production of oleanolic-acid-derived saponin aglycones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression with SvβAS in N. benthamiana and LC-MS detection of oleanolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SvβAS; SvC16 oxidase; SvC23-1; SvC23-2; AtATR1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio Iso-Seq BioProject PRJNA951399; Illumina 3'-Tag-RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA949801
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PB.8389.1 clustered with known C28 oxidases and was MeJA co-induced with SvβAS. 2. Co-expression with SvβAS in N. benthamiana produced oleanolic acid. 3. The gene was designated SvC28 oxidase/CYP716A173.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential oxidation module for engineering oleanolic acid-derived saponins in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Required substrate-generating step for both monodesmoside and bisdesmoside branches.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana; GAL/SepGAL promoters in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Candidate selected from MeJA-responsive CYPs; product outcome differed between N. benthamiana and yeast when combined with other oxidases
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Aglycone oxidation branch toward echinocystic acid/quillaic acid-type aglycones
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-tailoring step; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates oleanolic acid at C16α to form echinocystic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms precursor for quillaic acid-type bisdesmosidic saponins and hydroxylated monodesmoside aglycones
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Quillaic acid-related and C16-hydroxylated triterpenoid saponins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces C16α hydroxylation needed for formation of echinocystic acid and downstream quillaic acid-type products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with SvβAS and SvC28 oxidase; yeast validation with AtATR1
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SvβAS; SvC28 oxidase; SvC23-1; SvC23-2; AtATR1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio Iso-Seq BioProject PRJNA951399; Illumina 3'-Tag-RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA949801
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PB.29244.4, not the truncated PB.29244.1, encoded the active full-length C16 oxidase. 2. It converted oleanolic acid to echinocystic acid in planta and in yeast. 3. Combined with C23 oxidases, it contributed to mono- and bisdesmoside aglycone diversity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering quillaic acid-like aglycone structures and improving structural diversity of bioactive saponins
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Product spectrum is host dependent when combined with other oxidases
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PB.29244.1 contains an early stop codon and was not the functional enzyme.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana; GAL promoters in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA co-induced with SvβAS; product outcome altered by co-expression with SvCslG and by host system
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed and strongly co-upregulated with SvβAS by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Aglycone oxidation branch toward gypsogenic acid/gypsogenin derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-determining oxidation step with plasticity; not formally rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Oleanolic acid; likely C23 aldehyde intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Performs progressive C23 oxidation of oleanolic acid to gypsogenic acid; in some contexts also contributes to gypsogenin/quillaic acid-related products
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms aglycone branch used in monodesmosides and potentially bisdesmosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Gypsogenic acid- and quillaic acid-related triterpenoid saponins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Oxidizes oleanolic acid to gypsogenic acid; shows catalytic plasticity contributing to both mono- and bisdesmoside aglycone formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with SvβAS and SvC28 oxidase; yeast pathway validation; combinatorial co-expression with SvC16 and SvCslG
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SvβAS; SvC28 oxidase; SvC16 oxidase; SvCslG; AtATR1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio Iso-Seq BioProject PRJNA951399; Illumina 3'-Tag-RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA949801
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PB.33196.6 was identified as one of two active C23 oxidases. 2. It converted oleanolic acid to gypsogenic acid in N. benthamiana and yeast. 3. Product profile shifted in the presence of SvCslG, supporting a role in bisdesmoside-related aldehyde intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key branch enzyme for constructing monodesmoside and bisdesmoside pathways; useful with glucuronosyltransferase stacking to redirect product outcome
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Catalytic output is host- and context-dependent
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No hederagenin or gypsogenin accumulated detectably when expressed without SvCslG, indicating rapid overoxidation to gypsogenic acid.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana; GAL promoters in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA co-induced with SvβAS; product outcome altered by co-expression with SvCslG and by host system
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed and strongly co-upregulated with SvβAS by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Aglycone oxidation branch toward gypsogenic acid/gypsogenin derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-determining oxidation step with plasticity; not formally rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Oleanolic acid; likely C23 aldehyde intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Performs progressive C23 oxidation of oleanolic acid to gypsogenic acid; catalytic plasticity allows contribution to QA-related branch in combinations
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms aglycone branch used in monodesmosides and potentially bisdesmosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Gypsogenic acid- and quillaic acid-related triterpenoid saponins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Oxidizes oleanolic acid to gypsogenic acid and contributes to broader aglycone diversification in combination with other pathway enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with SvβAS and SvC28 oxidase; yeast pathway validation; combinatorial co-expression with SvC16 and SvCslG
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SvβAS; SvC28 oxidase; SvC16 oxidase; SvCslG; AtATR1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio Iso-Seq BioProject PRJNA951399; Illumina 3'-Tag-RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA949801
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PB.29444.23 was the second active C23 oxidase identified. 2. It produced gypsogenic acid from oleanolic acid in N. benthamiana and yeast. 3. Its oxidation profile could be redirected toward bisdesmoside-related intermediates by SvCslG.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative C23 oxidation module for flexible pathway engineering of saponin aglycones
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Catalytic output is host- and context-dependent
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A related candidate PB.29444.13 was inactive under tested conditions.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana; pESC-based yeast expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Candidate selected through co-upregulation with SvβAS; dosage effects tested by varying Agrobacterium OD
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves; microsomal expression in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream glycosylation step that redirects aglycone oxidation outcome
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for branch redirection from monodesmosides toward bisdesmosides
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Quillaic acid; gypsogenin; C16α-hydroxylated gypsogenic acid; UDP-GlcA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucuronic acid from UDP-GlcA to the C3 hydroxyl of triterpenoid aglycones, especially quillaic acid and C23 aldehyde intermediates
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification and branch redirection toward bisdesmosidic saponins
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Bisdesmosidic triterpenoid saponins with C3 glucuronic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Glucuronidates C3 of triterpenoid aglycones and shifts C23 oxidation product profile toward C23 aldehyde-containing bisdesmoside precursors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro microsomal assay with QA and UDP-GlcA; kinetics; transient expression in N. benthamiana; confocal localization; combinatorial pathway assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio Iso-Seq BioProject PRJNA951399; Illumina 3'-Tag-RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA949801
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SvCslG transfers GlcA to C3 of quillaic acid and other aglycones. 2. It is ER-associated and increases QA-C3-GlcA accumulation. 3. By glucuronidating C23 aldehyde intermediates before further oxidation, it redirects flux from monodesmoside-type products toward bisdesmosides.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target for generating bisdesmosidic saponins and controlling product profiles through enzyme colocalization and pathway channeling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May reduce formation of fully oxidized monodesmoside aglycones by intercepting aldehyde intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This Csl-type UGT is emphasized as necessary for efficient glucuronidation of particular triterpenoid aglycones, unlike soluble family 1 UGTs.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana; pESC-based yeast expression
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Identified after correcting incomplete isoform issue; PB.1747.1 itself was not co-upregulated with SvβAS but was inferred as full-length active isoform of same gene family
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: PB.41560.2 was co-upregulated with SvβAS but inactive; active full-length isoform PB.1747.1 not co-upregulated in RNA-seq list
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Important reinforcing step for C23 aldehyde-containing bisdesmoside formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose to the glucuronic acid residue at C3 of quillaic acid glucuronide
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for further assembly of C3 oligosaccharide in bisdesmosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Bisdesmosidic triterpenoid saponins with C3 GlcA-Gal chain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces QA-C3-GlcA-Gal and increases proportion of C23 aldehyde-containing C3-glycosylated products
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in QA-fed N. benthamiana with SvCslG; yeast validation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio Iso-Seq BioProject PRJNA951399; Illumina 3'-Tag-RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA949801
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The initially selected co-induced isoform PB.41560.2 was inactive due to truncation. 2. PB.1747.1 encoded the active full-length galactosyltransferase designated SvGalT/UGT73DL2. 3. Additional C3 glycosylation reinforced accumulation of QA-type bisdesmoside intermediates over more oxidized products.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for stepwise reconstruction of the C3 trisaccharide chain and improving desired QA-type saponin proportions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Requires upstream SvCslG-generated substrate; inactive truncated isoforms may complicate transcript-based candidate selection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper highlights isoform-level transcript resolution as critical for accurate functional assignment.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Identified by phylogenetic and co-upregulation analyses
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Important reinforcing step for QA-type bisdesmoside accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylose to the C3-linked GlcA-Gal sugar chain of quillaic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Builds terminal C3 trisaccharide branch of bisdesmosides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Bisdesmosidic triterpenoid saponins with C3 GlcA-Gal-Xyl chain
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Forms QA-C3-GlcA-Gal-Xyl and further increases the proportion of QA-type C23 aldehyde glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in QA-fed N. benthamiana with SvCslG and SvGalT
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio Iso-Seq BioProject PRJNA951399; Illumina 3'-Tag-RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA949801
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SvC3XylT was identified as a QA C3-chain xylosyltransferase. 2. It produced QA-C3-GlcA-Gal-Xyl in planta. 3. Along with SvGalT, it strongly boosted the relative abundance of QA-type over more oxidized C23 products.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Enables reconstruction of more native-like bisdesmoside C3 oligosaccharides in engineered hosts
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supports the idea that sequential C3 glycosylation reinforces aldehyde-intermediate capture.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UDP-glucose 4,6-dehydratase; homolog of N-terminal domain of AtRHM1
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Foundational step in newly elucidated UDP-D-fucose pathway; no rate limitation tested
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Dehydrates UDP-glucose to UDP-4-keto-6-deoxy-glucose, precursor of UDP-D-fucose
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for C28 fucosylation of triterpenoid saponins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates biosynthesis of UDP-D-fucose, enabling downstream fucosylation of saponins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with/without SvNMD and LC-MS detection of UDP-D-fucose/UDP-L-rhamnose
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio Iso-Seq BioProject PRJNA951399; Illumina 3'-Tag-RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA949801
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sv46DH is a MeJA-induced candidate for UDP-D-fucose biosynthesis. 2. Co-expression with SvNMD led to production of UDP-D-fucose in N. benthamiana. 3. It provides the upstream precursor for triterpenoid C28 fucosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical enabling gene for installing UDP-D-fucose supply in non-native hosts to produce fucosylated saponins such as QS-21-like molecules
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: When expressed alone, endogenous host enzymes divert intermediate toward UDP-L-rhamnose
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This work proposes the first plant UDP-D-fucose biosynthetic route.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal step creating UDP-D-fucose; no rate limitation tested
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms sugar donor for C28 fucosylation of triterpenoid saponins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces UDP-D-fucose from UDP-4K6DG and efficiently channels host intermediate toward D-fucose
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with/without Sv46DH and LC-MS detection of UDP-D-fucose
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio Iso-Seq BioProject PRJNA951399; Illumina 3'-Tag-RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA949801
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SvNMD was identified as the reductase partner for plant UDP-D-fucose biosynthesis. 2. Overexpression with GFP alone already yielded UDP-D-fucose by using endogenous host UDP-4K6DG. 3. Together with Sv46DH it establishes a complete donor pathway for C28 fucosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential donor-supply gene for engineering D-fucosylated natural products in heterologous systems
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May compete with endogenous nucleotide sugar fluxes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes unresolved possibility that SvNMD may also reduce 4-keto sugar after transfer to triterpenoid backbone.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Function required co-expression with Sv46DH and SvNMD for UDP-D-fucose supply
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not directly in co-upregulated list, but highly similar to co-expressed transcript PB.28124.2; chosen as active isoform candidate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Essential transfer step for C28 D-fucosylated saponins; no rate limitation tested
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a fucosyl residue to the C28 carboxyl group of QA-C3-GlcA, likely using UDP-D-fucose
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer and immunostimulatory bioactivities; structural relevance to QS-21-like molecules
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces QA-C3-GlcA-C28-Fuc when UDP-D-fucose pathway is supplied
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with SvCslG, Sv46DH, and SvNMD; LC-MS comparison with Q. saponaria FucT positive control
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: PacBio Iso-Seq BioProject PRJNA951399; Illumina 3'-Tag-RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA949801
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PB.12216.1 was validated as the S. vaccaria C28 fucosyltransferase candidate. 2. Formation of QA-C3-GlcA-C28-Fuc required both this enzyme and the UDP-D-fucose supply genes Sv46DH/SvNMD. 3. It provides the missing step for C28 D-fucosylated bisdesmoside assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for reconstructing QS-21-like and other high-value fucosylated saponins in engineered hosts
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Dependent on co-engineering of UDP-D-fucose supply; exact timing of keto reduction versus transfer remains unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Alternative splicing/isoform relationships complicated candidate selection.
